name: The Terra Cotta Vessel
description: Hand-thrown from rich ochre clay and fired at high temperature, this vessel features deeply etched botanical patterns inspired by ancient Southwestern motifs. A bold statement piece for any curated interior.

name: Obscura Angular Vase
description: Crafted in matte black stoneware with sharp geometric shoulders, this vase channels the quiet tension of modern sculpture. Its dark, light-absorbing surface creates a striking contrast against pale walls.

name: Azure Fracture Bottle
description: Glazed in layers of turquoise and teal, this squat bottle evokes sea-worn rock pools and tidal textures. Each piece emerges uniquely from the kiln, making every bottle a one-of-a-kind coastal artifact.

name: Ribbed Alabaster Pair
description: These creamy white vessels are slip-cast with rhythmic vertical ribbing that catches ambient light beautifully. Sold as a complementary duo, they bring quiet texture and understated elegance to any shelf or surface.

name: Charred Kintsugi Form
description: Inspired by the Japanese philosophy of finding beauty in imperfection, this dark-glazed vessel is hand-burnished and deliberately cracked, then repaired with gold-tinted slip. A philosophical object as much as a decorative one.

name: Rose Bloom Vessel
description: Striped in soft blush, lavender, and mint, this tall vase is wheel-thrown and hand-painted in a playful candy-stripe pattern. It brings a joyful, youthful energy to contemporary interiors without sacrificing craft.

name: Sagebrush Tall Pitcher
description: Thrown on a slow wheel and dried under desert sun, this tall pitcher carries earthy sage-green tones and a subtly uneven rim that speaks to human hands. Functional yet deeply beautiful, it holds flowers as gracefully as water.

name: Ivory Moon Jar
description: Modeled after the iconic Joseon-era moon jars, this generous spherical form is built from two half-spheres and left in a pure milky white glaze. Its asymmetry and warm luminosity make it a meditative presence in any room.

name: Copper Drip Flask
description: Iron-rich clay meets a flowing copper-oxide glaze in this dramatic flask form. The glaze runs and pools naturally during firing, creating molten rivers of amber and rust that are impossible to predict or replicate exactly.

name: Slate Pinch Bowl
description: Built entirely by hand without a wheel, this small bowl bears the honest fingerprint impressions of its maker. Finished in a cool slate-grey matte glaze, it is an intimate object — perfect as a ring dish or beside a candle.

name: Celadon Footed Cup
description: Inspired by classical Chinese celadon ware, this footed cup shimmers in a pale jade glaze over a delicate carved body. It is equally at home as a sake vessel or a small bud vase holding a single stem.

name: Obsidian Raku Bowl
description: Pulled from the kiln at peak temperature and smoked in a steel drum, this raku bowl carries a lustrous black surface with unpredictable crackle patterns. No two pieces share the same character — the fire decides the final form.

name: Blush Coil Vase
description: Built using the ancient coiling technique, this vase retains the subtle ridges of its construction as a design feature. Glazed in dusty rose with an unglazed lower half, it celebrates the raw material alongside refined surface.

name: Fossil Imprint Plate
description: Real botanical specimens are pressed into soft clay before firing, leaving ghostly leaf and fern impressions across this wide serving plate. Finished in a sandy buff glaze, it feels like a discovered artifact from another era.

name: Indigo Slab Vase
description: Hand-built from rolled slabs of white stoneware and dipped in a deep cobalt-indigo glaze, this angular vase challenges the expected roundness of ceramics. Its flat planes and sharp creases make it a sculptural object first.

name: Terracotta Flower Brick
description: Modeled after antique florist bricks, this low rectangular vessel has five cylindrical holes for arranging stems in Ikebana style. Its warm unglazed terracotta surface develops a natural patina with regular use and water.

name: White Porcelain Bud Vase
description: Thrown from translucent porcelain on an electric wheel and left unadorned, this slender bud vase celebrates the material itself. When held to light, the thinness of the walls glows warmly — a quiet meditation on simplicity.

name: Hammered Bronze Pot
description: A wood-fired pot whose exterior is brushed with layers of iron and manganese oxides, producing a surface that mimics hammered bronze. The interior reveals a contrasting pale slip glaze, creating a dramatic inside-outside dialogue.

name: Lavender Haze Bottle
description: A graceful bottle with a narrow neck and swollen body, glazed in shifting lavender and grey tones that change under different lighting conditions. Its smooth, cloud-like surface invites touch as much as admiration.

name: Cracked Earth Planter
description: The surface of this cylindrical planter is deliberately textured to resemble sun-cracked desert earth, achieved by applying a special crawling glaze. Drainage hole included, making it as practical as it is striking for indoor plants.

name: Sea Foam Yunomi
description: A Japanese-style yunomi tea cup glazed in frothy aquamarine and white, evoking ocean foam crashing on shore. Comfortable to hold with both hands, it transforms an everyday tea ritual into something quietly ceremonial.

name: Midnight Teapot
description: Wheel-thrown in dark iron-bearing clay and reduction fired, this teapot achieves a near-black surface with deep blue flashes visible in bright light. Its bamboo-wrapped handle and hand-formed spout reflect months of careful craft.

name: Sand Dune Sculpture
description: An abstract hand-built sculpture that mimics the rippled undulations of a sand dune, finished in a warm sandy bisque with subtle ochre highlights. It exists purely as art — no function beyond presence and contemplation.

name: Forest Green Amphora
description: Inspired by ancient Mediterranean storage vessels, this two-handled amphora is glazed in a deep forest green with subtle wood-ash drips along its shoulders. It bridges thousands of years of ceramic tradition with contemporary studio practice.

name: Ash White Lantern
description: Pierced with delicate geometric apertures and glazed in natural wood-ash white, this lantern vessel glows warmly when a tealight is placed inside. By day it is an elegant object; by night it becomes a source of soft, patterned light.

name: Russet Harvest Jug
description: A wide-bodied jug with a generous handle and pouring lip, glazed in warm russet and amber tones reminiscent of autumn harvest light. Fully functional for liquids and beautifully proportioned for display on open kitchen shelving.

name: Pebble Stack Trio
description: Three hand-formed vessels of graduating size, each smoothed to resemble a river pebble and glazed in warm grey and cream. Arranged together they create a meditative still life; separately each holds a candle, succulent, or small bouquet.

name: Emerald Faceted Bottle
description: Each face of this multifaceted bottle is carefully cut from thrown clay before firing, creating a gem-like silhouette. The rich emerald glaze pools differently in each carved plane, giving the surface the depth and complexity of precious stone.
